name: Illinois Pharmacy Technician License
description: Already licensed as a pharmacy technician? Here's whether your license transfers to Illinois - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.

provider.name: Illinois Board of Pharmacy
provider.parentOrganization: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation (IDFPR)

address.description: Illinois Department of Financial and Professional Regulation, 320 West Washington Street, Springfield, Illinois 62786

requiredDocuments.name: Official Transcripts
requiredDocuments.description: Sent directly from your educational institution.

requiredDocuments.name: Exam Score Report
requiredDocuments.description: Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

fingerprinting.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

mainEntity.name: How much does a Illinois pharmacy technician license cost?

acceptedAnswer.text: Illinois lists a $40 online application fee for pharmacy technician registration.
Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

mainEntity.name: Does Illinois have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

acceptedAnswer.text: No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Illinois. To work in Illinois, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

mainEntity.name: How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Illinois?

acceptedAnswer.text: Illinois does not publish a public pharmacy technician processing timeline in the application checklist. Check the IDFPR portal for status updates.

mainEntity.name: Can I start working in Illinois while my endorsement application is processing?

acceptedAnswer.text: Contact the Illinois Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Illinois.
